content.full_text:
June 9, 1976

Mr. Bruce Orvis
Secretary-Treasurer
Rock Creek Water District
Star Route, Box 49
Farmington, California 95230

Dear Mr. Orvis:

You indicate in your letter of May 20, 1976, that the
Rock Creek Water District is "only in Saanislaus County".
You also indicate that the District owns property in
Calaveras County. Government Code §82h11 provides:

"'Code reviewing body' means:

(a) The Commission, with respect to the Conflict
of Interest Code of ... any local government agency
with jurisdiction in more than one county;...."

Government Code §82035 provides:

"Real property shall be deemed to be 'within the
jurisdiction' with respect to a local government
agency if the property or any part of it is locat-
ed ... within two miles of any land owned or
used by the local government agency."

Based upon the fact that the District owns property in
Calaveras County, the District has jurisdiction in more than
one county within the meaning of Government Code §82035; and
accordingly, the Commission is the code reviewing body with
respect to the District. This determination is buttressed
by the apparent view of the Office of the Controller that the
Rock Creek Water District has jurisdiction in more than one
county. Accordingly, please submit the Conflict of Interest
Code of the Rock Creek Water District to this office on the
appropriate deadline date. Thank you.

Sincerely,

Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.
Chief, Conflicts of Interest
Division

/gib

COUNTY COUNSEL
FOURTH FLOOR, ADMINISTRATION BUILDING, 1221 OAK STREET RICHARD J. MOORE
OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 95612 TELEPHONE 874-6596 6851 COUNTY COUNSEL

August 30, 1976

Mr. Don Driggs
City Manager
City of Fremont
City Government Building
Fremont, California 94538

RE: Conflict of Interest Code

Dear Mr. Driggs:

Thank you for your letter of August 24, 1976, regarding
the code reviewing body for Fremont Unified School District.

We call your attention to the pertinent part of Section
82011 of the Government Code which reads as follows:

"Code reviewing body" means:

(a) The commission, with respect to the
Conflict of Interest Code of a state agency, a
county board of supervisors, a city council or any
local government agency with jurisdiction in more
than one county;

(b) The board of supervisors, with respect to
the Conflict of Interest Code of any county agency
other than the board of supervisors, and of any
local government agency, other than a city agency,
with jurisdiction wholly within the county;

and to Section 82041 which reads as follows:

"Local government agency" means a county, city
or district of any kind including school district,
or any other local or regional political subdivision,
or any department, division, bureau, office, board,
commission or other agency of these, but does not
include any court or any agency in the judicial branch
of government.

Our advice to the Fremont Unified School District that the
code reviewing body for it was the Board of Supervisors of Alameda

Mr. Don Driggs                                    August 30, 1976
                                                           Page 2


County was based upon these two sections as a

     "Local government agency" means a . . . district
     of any kind including school district.

Since the jurisdiction of the Fremont Unified School District is
wholly within the County of Alameda it falls within the provisions
of Section 82011, subdivision (b).

     If you have received advice to the contrary from the F.P.P.C.
it would be appreciated if you would contact it for clarification.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        RICHARD J. MOORE
                                        County Counsel

                                        By  [illegible]
                                            THOMAS E. FIRBY
                                            Assistant County Counsel

TEF:gk
